message.content:
POLARIS mission: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports — on EVERY axis.
The operator's directive: use ALL scorers; we must win on all, not cherry-pick the one we pass.

WORK ONLY IN /home/polaris/wt/compete. Reference (read-only): /workspace/POLARIS.
Competitor artifacts: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md — ONLY ChatGPT_Scoped, ChatGPT_Unscoped,
Gemini_Scoped, Gemini_Unscoped exist. NO FS-Researcher artifact (a background search is hunting the
FS-Researcher paper+benchmark separately) — report FSR as an open gap, never fake a third column.
Existing rendered POLARIS reports to score: find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md (~35 exist).

THE THREE AXES (orthogonal — a report can win one and lose another):
- DeepTRACE (EXISTS): scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py — 8 citation-faithfulness metrics.
  Judge lock config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml (signed, judge=moonshotai/kimi-k2.6,
  self_rescore fairness policy). Preflight deeptrace_judge_preflight.py HARD-BLOCKS if OPENROUTER_API_KEY
  is unset or provider headroom < 8. The key IS available: source it with
  `set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a` (NEVER print it).
- RACE (DOES NOT EXIST — must build): DeepResearch-Bench 4-dim reference-based LLM judge —
  Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, weighted by per-task criteria
  vs a reference answer. Build it honestly; do NOT relabel DeepTRACE as RACE.
- DRB-II (EXTERNAL): scripts/dr_benchmark/drbii_wrapper.py adapts the official DeepResearch-Bench-II
  rubric harness, but third_party/ is absent. Fetch it if fetchable; if not, say so plainly.

HARD HONESTY RULES:
- If POLARIS LOSES on an axis, say so and by how much. A flattering scoreboard is worse than none.
- Never tune a judge to make us win. Score every system with the SAME judge/tasks (self_rescore).
- Do NOT burn a fresh 346-basket render — score the ~35 reports that already exist.
- Cost/rate: paid judge calls on a SHARED OpenRouter account (~217 credits left). If you see HTTP 429,
  SAY SO in blockers; do not silently retry forever. Never print the API key.


AXIS 1 — DEEPTRACE (faithfulness), the one that already exists. Make it RUN.
1. Source the key: `set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a` then run
   deeptrace_judge_preflight.py — confirm it now PASSES (it hard-blocked before only because the key
   was unset).
2. Score a substantive real POLARIS report (find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md) AND all 4
   competitor artifacts with the SAME kimi-k2.6 judge (self_rescore). Watch cost — one report is
   ~178 support calls; start with ONE POLARIS + the 4 competitors, not all 35.
3. Report the 8-metric faithfulness scoreboard, POLARIS vs each competitor, and who wins per metric.
   If 429s appear, log them and stop rather than burning credits.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me look at the DeepTRACE scorer, preflight, and judge lock config.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-deepfix-001 fix I5 — DeepTRACE benchmark-judge provider/headroom preflight (the measurement gate).
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS (the measurement precondition, closes the Codex iter-2 novel P1)
5	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6	The DeepTRACE 8-metric scorer (``scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py``) needs a binary
7	Factual-Support Matrix ``S[i][j] = "does listed source j support statement i?"``. That per-(statement,
8	source) decision is made by a BENCHMARK-SCORER judge locked SEPARATELY from the runtime faithfulness
9	judge in ``config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml``. A drb_72-class report makes ~178 statement-
10	support calls under concurrency, so the locked judge MUST have real OpenRouter provider redundancy — a
11	low-provider model 429s and the support matrix comes back PARTIAL, which silently produces a WRONG
12	DeepTRACE number. And a number scored under an UNSIGNED lock is not an operator-authorized measurement.
13	
14	THIS PREFLIGHT FAILS LOUD (refuses to let any DeepTRACE number be claimed) when ANY of:
15	  1. the lock is UNSIGNED (``signature.signed`` is not true) — the lock is inert until the operator signs.
16	  2. the locked benchmark judge has FEWER operational OpenRouter providers than
17	     ``provider_headroom.min_operational_provider_count`` — the support matrix would return partial.
18	  3. provider headroom could NOT be measured (no ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` / network error) — never a
19	     silent pass; an unmeasured headroom is a blocker, not an OK.
20	  4. the comparability policy is missing or not one of the recognized honest paths — a number produced
21	     under an undeclared comparability policy is not comparable and must not be claimed.
22	
23	DNA: this is a MEASUREMENT gate + measurement-judge lock, not a pipeline knob. It has NO cap / target /
24	thinner on the pipeline; it only decides whether a DeepTRACE NUMBER is claimable. Fail-loud, never a
25	silent fallback (LAW II). Every threshold comes from the operator-signed lock (LAW VI).
26	
27	BUILD-ONLY / OFFLINE-TESTABLE
28	-----------------------------
29	``evaluate_preflight(lock, operational_provider_count)`` is a PURE function over a lock dict + an integer
30	count (offline, $0, no network) — that is what the unit test exercises RED->GREEN. Only ``--check`` touches
31	the network (a single GET to OpenRouter ``/models/{slug}/endpoints`` to count operational providers, using
32	the SAME operational definition as ``scripts/diagnostics/rank_openrouter_providers.py``: ``status == 0``
33	AND ``uptime_last_30m >= PG_PROVIDER_UPTIME_FLOOR``).
34	
35	Usage
36	-----
37	    # offline: evaluate the lock with a known provider count (e.g. from a prior --check)
38	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --provider-count 21
39	
40	    # live: count operational providers for the locked judge, then evaluate (exit 0 clean, 4 == blocked)
41	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check
42	"""
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import argparse
46	import json
47	import os
48	import sys
49	import urllib.request
50	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
51	from typing import Any, Mapping, Optional
52	
53	# The default DeepTRACE benchmark-judge lock path (LAW VI: overridable via --lock).
54	DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH = "config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml"
55	
56	# Operational-endpoint definition, IDENTICAL to scripts/diagnostics/rank_openrouter_providers.py so the
57	# headroom count matches the routing layer's notion of "healthy". LAW VI: env-overridable floor.
58	_UPTIME_FLOOR = float(os.getenv("PG_PROVIDER_UPTIME_FLOOR", "98.0"))
59	
60	# The honest comparability policies the lock may declare (arXiv 2509.04499 measurement discipline).
61	_VALID_COMPARABILITY_POLICIES = {"paper_judge", "self_rescore"}
62	
63	
64	@dataclass
65	class PreflightResult:
66	    """The outcome of the DeepTRACE benchmark-judge preflight. ``claimable`` is True IFF a DeepTRACE
67	    number may be claimed (lock signed, provider headroom met + measured, comparability declared)."""
68	
69	    claimable: bool
70	    blockers: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
71	    signed: bool = False
72	    model_slug: Optional[str] = None
73	    min_provider_floor: Optional[int] = None
74	    operational_provider_count: Optional[int] = None
75	    comparability_policy: Optional[str] = None
76	
77	
78	def load_lock(path: str = DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH) -> dict:
79	    """Load the benchmark-judge lock YAML. Raises FileNotFoundError (fail-loud) if it is missing — a
80	    DeepTRACE number cannot be claimed without the operator-signed lock present."""
81	    import yaml  # local import: keep the module import lightweight for the pure path
82	
83	    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
84	        data = yaml.safe_load(fh)
85	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
86	        raise ValueError(f"{path}: expected a YAML mapping, got {type(data).__name__}")
87	    return data
88	
89	
90	def _as_bool(value: Any) -> bool:
91	    if isinstance(value, bool):
92	        return value
93	    return str(value).strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
94	
95	
96	def evaluate_preflight(
97	    lock: Mapping[str, Any],
98	    operational_provider_count: Optional[int],
99	) -> PreflightResult:
100	    """PURE preflight (offline, $0). Decide whether a DeepTRACE number is claimable for the given lock +
101	    measured operational provider count. ``operational_provider_count is None`` means "could not measure"
102	    and is itself a blocker (never a silent pass).
103	
104	    Blockers (fail-loud, LAW II):
105	      * lock unsigned;
106	      * comparability policy missing / unrecognized;
107	      * provider headroom unmeasured (count is None);
108	      * operational provider count below the lock's ``min_operational_provider_count`` floor.
109	    """
110	    blockers: list[str] = []
111	
112	    signature = lock.get("signature") or {}
113	    signed = _as_bool(signature.get("signed", False))
114	    if not signed:
115	        blockers.append(
116	            "benchmark-judge lock is UNSIGNED (signature.signed is not true) — the lock is INERT until "
117	            "an operator signs it; NO DeepTRACE number is claimable. Sign "
118	            f"{DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH} (signature.signed:true + operator + signed_commit)."
119	        )
120	
121	    benchmark_judge = lock.get("benchmark_judge") or {}
122	    model_slug = benchmark_judge.get("model_slug")
123	    if not model_slug:
124	        blockers.append("benchmark_judge.model_slug is empty — the benchmark judge is not declared.")
125	
126	    comparability = lock.get("comparability") or {}
127	    policy = comparability.get("policy")
128	    if policy not in _VALID_COMPARABILITY_POLICIES:
129	        blockers.append(
130	            f"comparability.policy={policy!r} is not one of {sorted(_VALID_COMPARABILITY_POLICIES)} — a "
131	            "DeepTRACE number produced under an undeclared comparability policy is not comparable and "
132	            "must not be claimed (never mix self_rescore POLARIS numbers with paper-published numbers)."
133	        )
134	
135	    headroom = lock.get("provider_headroom") or {}
136	    try:
137	        min_floor = int(headroom.get("min_operational_provider_count"))
138	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
139	        min_floor = None
140	    if min_floor is None:
141	        blockers.append(
142	            "provider_headroom.min_operational_provider_count is missing/invalid — the headroom floor "
143	            "is undefined, so headroom cannot be enforced."
144	        )
145	
146	    if operational_provider_count is None:
147	        blockers.append(
148	            "provider headroom could NOT be measured (no OPENROUTER_API_KEY or a network error on the "
149	            "/models/{slug}/endpoints probe) — an UNMEASURED headroom is a blocker, never a silent pass. "
150	            "Run --check with OPENROUTER_API_KEY set."
151	        )
152	    elif min_floor is not None and operational_provider_count < min_floor:
153	        blockers.append(
154	            f"benchmark judge {model_slug!r} has {operational_provider_count} operational OpenRouter "
155	            f"provider(s) < the lock floor of {min_floor} — the ~"
156	            f"{headroom.get('expected_statement_support_calls_per_report', '?')} statement-support "
157	            "calls/report would 429 and the Factual-Support Matrix would return PARTIAL -> a wrong "
158	            "DeepTRACE number. Raise headroom (a higher-provider judge) or lower the operator-signed "
159	            "floor before claiming a number."
160	        )
161	
162	    return PreflightResult(
163	        claimable=not blockers,
164	        blockers=blockers,
165	        signed=signed,
166	        model_slug=model_slug,
167	        min_provider_floor=min_floor,
168	        operational_provider_count=operational_provider_count,
169	        comparability_policy=policy,
170	    )
171	
172	
173	def count_operational_providers(model_slug: str, timeout: float = 30.0) -> Optional[int]:
174	    """Count OpenRouter endpoints for ``model_slug`` that are OPERATIONAL (status == 0 AND
175	    uptime_last_30m >= PG_PROVIDER_UPTIME_FLOOR). Returns None on any network/auth failure (the caller
176	    treats None as a fail-loud blocker — never a silent 0-or-pass). Network; only called by --check."""
177	    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
178	    if not api_key:
179	        return None
180	    url = f"https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models/{model_slug}/endpoints"
181	    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}"})
182	    try:
183	        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
184	            payload = json.loads(resp.read())
185	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — any probe failure => unmeasured (None), a fail-loud blocker upstream
186	        return None
187	    endpoints = (payload.get("data") or {}).get("endpoints") or []
188	    operational = 0
189	    for ep in endpoints:
190	        up = ep.get("uptime_last_30m")
191	        if ep.get("status") == 0 and (up is None or up >= _UPTIME_FLOOR):
192	            operational += 1
193	    return operational
194	
195	
196	def _print_result(result: PreflightResult) -> None:
197	    print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] benchmark judge:", result.model_slug)
198	    print(f"[deeptrace-judge-preflight] lock signed: {result.signed}")
199	    print(
200	        "[deeptrace-judge-preflight] operational providers:",
201	        result.operational_provider_count,
202	        f"(floor {result.min_provider_floor})",
203	    )
204	    print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] comparability policy:", result.comparability_policy)
205	    if result.claimable:
206	        print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] OK — a DeepTRACE number is claimable under this lock.")
207	    else:
208	        print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] BLOCKED — a DeepTRACE number is NOT claimable:")
209	        for b in result.blockers:
210	            print(f"  - {b}")
211	
212	
213	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
214	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
215	        description="DeepTRACE benchmark-judge provider/headroom preflight (I5 measurement gate)."
216	    )
217	    ap.add_argument("--lock", default=DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH, help="path to the benchmark-judge lock YAML")
218	    ap.add_argument(
219	        "--check",
220	        action="store_true",
221	        help="LIVE: count operational OpenRouter providers for the locked judge, then evaluate",
222	    )
223	    ap.add_argument(
224	        "--provider-count",
225	        type=int,
226	        default=None,
227	        help="offline: evaluate against a KNOWN operational-provider count (skips the network probe)",
228	    )
229	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
230	
231	    try:
232	        lock = load_lock(args.lock)
233	    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as exc:
234	        print(f"[deeptrace-judge-preflight] BLOCKED — cannot load lock: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
235	        return 4
236	
237	    count: Optional[int] = args.provider_count
238	    if args.check:
239	        model_slug = (lock.get("benchmark_judge") or {}).get("model_slug") or ""
240	        count = count_operational_providers(model_slug) if model_slug else None
241	
242	    result = evaluate_preflight(lock, count)
243	    _print_result(result)
244	    return 0 if result.claimable else 4
245	
246	
247	if __name__ == "__main__":
248	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""I-deepfix-001 WS-14 — DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness scorer (re-implementation).
2	
3	DeepTRACE (Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499, ICLR 2026) defines 8 audit metrics over a report's
4	statements x sources. There is NO public official scorer/leaderboard, so this is a RE-IMPLEMENTATION whose
5	output is an ESTIMATE, not a proven score: the paper's judge is GPT-5 (Pearson 0.62 vs human); we substitute
6	our D8 kimi-k2.6 judge to build the support matrix (DISCLOSED). Calibrate against the paper's published
7	GPT-5-Deep-Research column before claiming a rank.
8	
9	Inputs (built upstream from the rendered report):
10	  * citation_matrix  C : list[list[int]] of shape [n_statements][n_sources]; C[i][j]=1 iff statement i
11	    cites source j.
12	  * support_matrix   S : list[list[int]] of the same shape; S[i][j]=1 iff source j's content SUPPORTS
13	    statement i (per the judge).
14	  * per-statement metadata: relevant (bool), confidence (int 1-5 or None), stance ('pro'|'con'|'neutral').
15	
16	Exact formulas (paper section on metrics):
17	  III Relevant Statements   = relevant / total
18	  IV  Uncited Sources       = cited / listed           (we also report uncited = 1 - cited/listed)
19	  V   Unsupported Statements = unsupported / relevant   (a relevant statement with no supporting source)
20	  VII Citation Accuracy     = sum(C (x) S) / sum(C)
21	  VIII Citation Thoroughness = sum(C (x) S) / sum(S)
22	  I   One-Sided  (debate)   = 1 iff NOT (>=1 pro AND >=1 con) relevant statement
23	  II  Overconfident (debate)= 1 iff One-Sided AND answer confidence == 5
24	  VI  Source Necessity      = size_of_minimum_source_cover / listed, where the minimum source cover is the
25	      FEWEST sources whose union still SUPPORTS every supported relevant statement (paper 2509.04499
26	      "minimum vertex cover for source nodes"; the official answer-engine-eval reference uses greedy set
27	      cover, which we match — no exact Hopcroft-Karp needed). A statement supported by 2 sources therefore
28	      contributes cover-size 1 (necessity 0.5 of 2 listed), NOT 0. The old SOLE-supporter count (a source
29	      that is the only supporter of some supported relevant statement) is retained as a SECONDARY
30	      disclosure field ``n_sole_supporter`` — it is a lower bound on the cover size, nothing is lost.
31	"""
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	from typing import Any, Optional, Sequence
35	
36	
37	def _sum_hadamard(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]], b: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
38	    """sum(a (x) b) — element-wise product summed over the whole matrix."""
39	    total = 0
40	    for row_a, row_b in zip(a, b):
41	        for x, y in zip(row_a, row_b):
42	            total += int(bool(x)) * int(bool(y))
43	    return total
44	
45	
46	def _sum_matrix(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
47	    return sum(int(bool(x)) for row in a for x in row)
48	
49	
50	def _cited_source_columns(citation_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]], n_sources: int) -> set[int]:
51	    cited: set[int] = set()
52	    for row in citation_matrix:
53	        for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)):
54	            if row[j]:
55	                cited.add(j)
56	    return cited
57	
58	
59	def necessary_source_count(
60	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
61	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
62	    n_sources: int,
63	) -> int:
64	    """SECONDARY disclosure (NOT the DeepTRACE VI numerator anymore): count sources that are the SOLE
65	    supporter of at least one relevant statement. Such a source must appear in every cover, so this count
66	    is a LOWER BOUND on the minimum source cover size (see ``minimum_source_cover_size``). Redundantly-
67	    supported statements contribute no sole supporter; a statement supported by exactly one source makes
68	    that source a sole supporter. Retained so nothing is lost after the min-cover fix."""
69	    necessary: set[int] = set()
70	    for i, row in enumerate(support_matrix):
71	        if i >= len(relevant) or not relevant[i]:
72	            continue
73	        supporters = [j for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)) if row[j]]
74	        if len(supporters) == 1:
75	            necessary.add(supporters[0])
76	    return len(necessary)
77	
78	
79	def minimum_source_cover_size(
80	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
81	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
82	    n_sources: int,
83	) -> int:
84	    """DeepTRACE VI numerator: the size of the MINIMUM source cover over the supported relevant statements.
85	
86	    universe = indices of relevant statements that have >= 1 supporting source (i.e. supported).
87	    sets     = for each listed source j, the set of supported relevant statements it supports (S[i][j]==1).
88	    The cover = the fewest sources whose union covers the whole universe. Computed by greedy set cover
89	    (repeatedly take the source covering the most still-uncovered statements), which is the standard
90	    reference implementation used by the official answer-engine-eval code — exact Hopcroft-Karp is not
91	    required. Deterministic tie-break: lowest source index. A statement supported by 2 sources yields a
92	    cover of size 1 (not 0), fixing the sole-supporter divergence. Returns 0 for an empty universe."""
93	    universe: set[int] = set()
94	    source_sets: dict[int, set[int]] = {j: set() for j in range(n_sources)}
95	    for i, row in enumerate(support_matrix):
96	        if i >= len(relevant) or not relevant[i]:
97	            continue
98	        supporters = [j for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)) if row[j]]
99	        if not supporters:  # unsupported relevant statement is not in the cover universe
100	            continue
101	        universe.add(i)
102	        for j in supporters:
103	            source_sets[j].add(i)
104	
105	    uncovered = set(universe)
106	    cover_size = 0
107	    while uncovered:
108	        best_j: Optional[int] = None
109	        best_cov = 0
110	        for j in range(n_sources):
111	            cov = len(source_sets[j] & uncovered)
112	            if cov > best_cov:
113	                best_cov = cov
114	                best_j = j
115	        if best_j is None:  # no source covers a remaining statement (unreachable: each has a supporter)
116	            break
117	        uncovered -= source_sets[best_j]
118	        cover_size += 1
119	    return cover_size
120	
121	
122	def _confidence_is_overconfident(
123	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
124	    statement_confidence: Sequence[Optional[int]],
125	) -> bool:
126	    """The answer is confidence-5 if an explicit answer confidence is 5, else if the max per-statement
127	    confidence is 5 (the paper's confidence scoring is statement-level)."""
128	    if answer_confidence is not None:
129	        return int(answer_confidence) == 5
130	    vals = [int(c) for c in statement_confidence if c is not None]
131	    return bool(vals) and max(vals) == 5
132	
133	
134	def compute_deeptrace_metrics(
135	    *,
136	    citation_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
137	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
138	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
139	    n_sources: int,
140	    stance: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None,
141	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
142	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
143	    is_debate: bool = False,
144	) -> dict[str, Any]:
145	    """Compute all 8 DeepTRACE metrics. Debate-only metrics (One-Sided, Overconfident) are None when
146	    ``is_debate`` is False. Ratios are 0.0 when their denominator is 0 (an empty answer). PURE / offline."""
147	    n_statements = len(citation_matrix)
148	    total = n_statements
149	    n_relevant = sum(1 for i in range(total) if i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
150	
151	    # III Relevant Statements
152	    relevant_ratio = (n_relevant / total) if total else 0.0
153	
154	    # IV Uncited Sources (report cited fraction + its complement)
155	    cited_cols = _cited_source_columns(citation_matrix, n_sources)
156	    cited_fraction = (len(cited_cols) / n_sources) if n_sources else 0.0
157	    uncited_fraction = (1.0 - cited_fraction) if n_sources else 0.0
158	
159	    # V Unsupported Statements (over RELEVANT statements)
160	    unsupported = 0
161	    for i in range(total):
162	        if i < len(relevant) and relevant[i]:
163	            row = support_matrix[i] if i < len(support_matrix) else []
164	            if not any(row[j] for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources))):
165	                unsupported += 1
166	    unsupported_ratio = (unsupported / n_relevant) if n_relevant else 0.0
167	
168	    # VII / VIII Citation Accuracy / Thoroughness
169	    cs = _sum_hadamard(citation_matrix, support_matrix)
170	    sum_c = _sum_matrix(citation_matrix)
171	    sum_s = _sum_matrix(support_matrix)
172	    citation_accuracy = (cs / sum_c) if sum_c else 0.0
173	    citation_thoroughness = (cs / sum_s) if sum_s else 0.0
174	
175	    # VI Source Necessity = min-source-cover size / listed (paper "minimum vertex cover for source nodes").
176	    cover_size = minimum_source_cover_size(support_matrix, relevant, n_sources)
177	    source_necessity = (cover_size / n_sources) if n_sources else 0.0
178	    # SECONDARY disclosure retained so nothing is lost: the old sole-supporter count (lower bound on cover).
179	    n_sole_supporter = necessary_source_count(support_matrix, relevant, n_sources)
180	
181	    # I / II debate-only
182	    one_sided: Optional[int] = None
183	    overconfident: Optional[int] = None
184	    if is_debate:
185	        st = list(stance or [])
186	        has_pro = any(
187	            (i < len(st) and st[i] == "pro") and (i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
188	            for i in range(total)
189	        )
190	        has_con = any(
191	            (i < len(st) and st[i] == "con") and (i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
192	            for i in range(total)
193	        )
194	        one_sided = 0 if (has_pro and has_con) else 1
195	        conf5 = _confidence_is_overconfident(
196	            answer_confidence, list(statement_confidence or [None] * total)
197	        )
198	        overconfident = 1 if (one_sided == 1 and conf5) else 0
199	
200	    return {
201	        "n_statements": total,
202	        "n_relevant_statements": n_relevant,
203	        "n_listed_sources": n_sources,
204	        # lower-better
205	        "one_sided": one_sided,
206	        "overconfident": overconfident,
207	        "uncited_sources_fraction": round(uncited_fraction, 4),
208	        "unsupported_statements_ratio": round(unsupported_ratio, 4),
209	        # higher-better
210	        "relevant_statements_ratio": round(relevant_ratio, 4),
211	        "source_necessity": round(source_necessity, 4),
212	        "source_necessity_cover_size": cover_size,
213	        "n_sole_supporter": n_sole_supporter,
214	        "citation_accuracy": round(citation_accuracy, 4),
215	        "citation_thoroughness": round(citation_thoroughness, 4),
216	        # provenance / honesty
217	        "scorer": "polaris-reimpl-of-deeptrace",
218	        "is_estimate": True,
219	        "judge_substitution": "kimi-k2.6 (paper uses GPT-5) — DISCLOSED",
220	        "source_necessity_interpretation": (
221	            "min-source-cover (greedy set cover over supported relevant statements) / n_listed_sources; "
222	            "n_sole_supporter is the retained sole-supporter lower bound"
223	        ),
224	    }

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Pack a POLARIS report into the official DeepTRACE (answer-engine-eval) input schema:
one record per task = {id, Question, Output, S1..S10}. The official scorer
(third_party/answer-engine-eval/Venkit.et.al.2024/populate_scores.py) then scrapes each
S{i} via r.jina.ai, gpt-4o-extracts core statements, and judges (statement, source) support.

THE 10-SOURCE CAP (code-confirmed: populate_scores.py uses `range(1,11)` x3; the body's `[N]`
markers are parsed by utils_misc.extract_citations as source indices N). A POLARIS report
cites hundreds of sources, so this packer reduces the body to a MINIMAL load-bearing set of
<=10 sources via the harness's OWN greedy_set_cover objective (DeepTRACE source-necessity #6),
and RENUMBERS the body citations to `[1..k]`, k<=10. Statements whose only sources are dropped
become uncited (honest — we do not invent support). The full keep-all corroboration set is NOT
listed here (it lives in POLARIS's own bibliography/sidecar, scored by DRB-II independence).

INVARIANTS (eval integrity):
  - NEVER pre-fills S{i}_content (the official scorer scrapes it; pre-filling POLARIS's own
    spans would be the §-1.1 self-serving-eval shortcut — forbidden).
  - Output is the cleaned answer body (masthead/base64/gap-stub stripped via pack_drb2.strip_junk,
    [#ev:] provenance tokens removed, appendix dropped) — never POLARIS-internal meta.
  - Faithfulness untouched: this packs/renumbers existing citations; it judges nothing.
LAW VI: caps are env-overridable.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import sys
from collections import defaultdict
from pathlib import Path

_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO / "scripts" / "dr_benchmark"))
import pack_drb2  # noqa: E402  (reuse strip_junk / answer_body)

MAX_SOURCES = int(os.getenv("PACK_DEEPTRACE_MAX_SOURCES", "10"))  # the official range(1,11) cap

_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[([\d,\s]+)\]")          # [N] / [N, M] numbered citations
_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")      # POLARIS-internal provenance tokens (stripped)
# Intra-line sentence boundary (applied AFTER the body is split into lines): POLARIS's
# trailing-citation prose style `... end.[1] Next ...[2]`. Fixed-width lookbehind (1-char class).
_INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?\]])\s+(?=[A-Z0-9])")


def _split_statements(body: str) -> list[str]:
    """Split a report body into the FINEST citation-bearing units for the source-necessity
    matrix. Each Markdown LINE is its own block, so table rows / list items / headings / block
    quotes never collapse into one another (the Codex P1 class — first lists, then tables); a
    table row (`|`-led) is further split per-cell on `|` so two cells citing two sources stay
    distinct; a prose line is split into sentences on terminal punctuation.

    DIRECTIONAL SAFETY: over-splitting is harmless for greedy set-cover (a source that uniquely
    covers any unit is still selected — it never DROPS a load-bearing source); only UNDER-splitting
    (collapsing distinct cited units) drops sources. This matrix is INTERNAL source-selection only;
    the official scorer re-extracts statements from `Output` itself, so granularity here never
    changes what the judge reads — it only makes the <=10 kept sources strictly more conservative."""

    for line in body.splitlines():

2	"""Offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — I-deepfix-001 Wave-1c (#1344), TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY.
3	
4	================================  READ THIS FIRST  ================================
5	This script ESTIMATES a POLARIS rendered report's DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness score
6	OFFLINE, before spending on the paid GPT-5 / kimi-k2.6 judge. It is a **TRIAGE PREDICTOR,
7	NOT a pass/fail gate.** It never aborts, raises on bad input, blocks, or decides anything by
8	a hardcoded threshold (LAW VI). Its numbers are ESTIMATES, not a proven score. Per operator
9	directive ("prove our own scorer correct then trust it") the point of this module is
10	formula-fidelity: every metric below is a small pure function whose docstring quotes the exact
11	DeepTRACE formula so a reviewer can verify it. It does NOT replace the official paid harness —
12	it predicts it, cheaply, so a shallow/broken report can be caught before paid judging.
13	
14	DeepTRACE = Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499. The 8 metrics are computed over a
15	report's statements × sources via a Citation matrix C and a Factual-support matrix F.
16	
17	HONEST LIMITATION — THE F-MATRIX IS SPAN-APPROXIMATE BY CONSTRUCTION
18	-------------------------------------------------------------------
19	DeepTRACE judges F over each source's FULL fetched text. We only have the banked
20	`corpus_snapshot.json` EXTRACTED SPANS (`direct_quote`) — median ~2.8K of ~14.7K chars,
21	~550/694 sources truncated (per REAL_PLAN_2026). So a statement that IS truly supported by a
22	source whose supporting sentence was not captured in the span reads as UNSUPPORTED here.
23	Net direction: this scorer UNDER-estimates support → OVER-estimates Unsupported-Statements,
24	UNDER-estimates Citation-Thoroughness. Sources whose bibliography URL/title resolves to no
25	snapshot span are `n_unreachable` (like DeepTRACE dropping ~15% un-scrapeable URLs) and can
26	never contribute F-support; they only DEFLATE Source-Necessity. Confidence ranking of the
27	predictions (stated so the reader weights them correctly):
28	  * BEST predicted (still span-approximate): Citation-Accuracy, Citation-Thoroughness,
29	    Unsupported-Statements, Uncited-Sources.
30	  * WEAK / PROXY: Relevant-Statement — core = an offline citation-presence PROXY for the paid
31	    GPT-5 core/filler `relevant[]` labels (unavailable offline); it WILL diverge from the paid
32	    Relevant-Statement and is the least-reliable estimate (flagged relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy).
33	  * WEAKEST / usually N/A offline: One-Sided, Overconfident (need a stance + 1-5 confidence
34	    judge we do not run offline; None unless the caller supplies the labels).
35	
36	SOURCE-NECESSITY — FORMULA-FIDELITY CONFLICT (flagged for the Codex+Fable gate)
37	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	The Wave-1c task text names "Hopcroft-Karp → König min-VERTEX-cover". The primary sources use
39	min-SET-cover, and a triage predictor MUST compute the same quantity the paid path computes:
40	  * `scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py` (POLARIS paid path) = greedy min-SET-cover.
41	  * `third_party/answer-engine-eval/.../utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover` (official reference) =
42	    greedy set cover.
43	König two-sided min-vertex-cover (= max-matching size) OVER-states necessity on redundant
44	reports (statements {s1,s2,s3}; sources a={s1,s2}, b={s2,s3}, c={s1,s3}: set cover 2 → 2/3
45	correct, but max matching 3 → König 3 → 1.0 wrong). So the HEADLINE `source_necessity` uses
46	greedy min-set-cover (valid predictor), and Hopcroft-Karp→König min-vertex-cover is kept as the
47	LABELED diagnostic `source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic` (the algorithm the task names, implemented
48	+ tested). See `.codex/I-deepfix-001/wave1c_brief.md`.
49	
50	Fully disjoint: this script imports nothing from POLARIS except the NLI engine
51	`entails_directional` (imported LAZILY inside the default entailment wrapper, so importing this
52	module and `--help` never load torch / sentence-transformers). It modifies NO existing module.
53	"""

93	# (BESTPRACTICE_2026_BRIEF DEEP PACK 1 / arXiv 2509.04499). C=citation matrix,
94	# F=factual-support matrix, ⊙=element-wise product, Σ=sum over all cells.

96	def one_sided_answer(has_pro: bool, has_con: bool) -> int:
97	    """Metric I — One-Sided (binary, debate queries only). LOWER better.
98	
99	    Formula: 0 if BOTH a pro AND a con statement are present, else 1.
100	    Offline we have no stance classifier; the caller supplies has_pro / has_con (else the
101	    orchestrator reports None = N/A)."""
102	    return 0 if (has_pro and has_con) else 1
103

105	def _confidence_is_overconfident(
106	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
107	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
108	) -> bool:
109	    """Whole-answer confidence==5 test — replicated EXACTLY from the paid path
110	    `scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py:_confidence_is_overconfident` (lines 122-131) so the
111	    triage Overconfident prediction matches the paid metric. If an explicit whole-answer
112	    confidence is provided it is AUTHORITATIVE (==5); OTHERWISE fall back to whether the MAX
113	    per-statement 1-5 confidence is 5 (the paper scores confidence statement-level). Exact
114	    short-circuit: a PROVIDED answer_confidence takes precedence and the statement branch is only
115	    consulted when answer_confidence is None (verified against the paid helper in the test)."""
116	    if answer_confidence is not None:
117	        return int(answer_confidence) == 5
118	    vals = [int(c) for c in (statement_confidence or []) if c is not None]
119	    return bool(vals) and max(vals) == 5
120

122	def overconfident_answer(
123	    one_sided: int,
124	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
125	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
126	) -> int:
127	    """Metric II — Overconfident (binary, debate queries only). LOWER better.
128	
129	    Formula: 1 if (One-Sided==1 AND whole-answer Confidence==5) else 0, where the confidence-5
130	    test is the paid path's `_confidence_is_overconfident` (answer_confidence==5, else max
131	    per-statement confidence==5). Confidence is a 1-5 Likert from a separate judge pass; None
132	    offline unless the caller supplies it."""
133	    return 1 if (one_sided == 1 and _confidence_is_overconfident(
134	        answer_confidence, statement_confidence)) else 0

137	def relevant_statement_ratio(n_core: int, n_total: int) -> float:
138	    """Metric III — Relevant Statement (ratio). HIGHER better.
139	
140	    Formula: (# relevant/core statements) / (total statements). 0.0 on empty."""
141	    return (n_core / n_total) if n_total else 0.0

144	def uncited_sources_ratio(n_cited: int, n_listed: int) -> float:
145	    """Metric IV — Uncited Sources (ratio, reported as %uncited). LOWER better.
146	
147	    Formula: (# listed − # cited) / (# listed) — the fraction of listed sources that NO
148	    statement cites. (The complement, cited/listed, is reported alongside.) 0.0 on empty."""
149	    return ((n_listed - n_cited) / n_listed) if n_listed else 0.0
150

152	def unsupported_statements_ratio(n_unsupported: int, n_relevant: int) -> float:
153	    """Metric V — Unsupported Statements (ratio). LOWER better.
154	
155	    Formula: (# unsupported statements) / (# relevant statements), where a relevant statement is
156	    unsupported iff NO listed source supports it in F (its F row is all-zero). 0.0 on empty."""
157	    return (n_unsupported / n_relevant) if n_relevant else 0.0

160	def source_necessity_ratio(cover_size: int, n_listed: int) -> float:
161	    """Metric VI — Source Necessity (ratio). HIGHER better.
162	
163	    Formula: (# necessary sources = |minimum source cover|) / (# listed sources). The HEADLINE
164	    cover is the greedy min-SET-cover over supported relevant statements (matches the paid
165	    `deeptrace_scorer.py` and the official `utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover`). A Hopcroft-Karp →
166	    König min-VERTEX-cover value is reported separately as a labeled diagnostic. 0.0 on empty."""
167	    return (cover_size / n_listed) if n_listed else 0.0

170	def citation_accuracy(sum_c_and_f: int, sum_c: int) -> float:
171	    """Metric VII — Citation Accuracy (= precision). HIGHER better.
172	
173	    Formula: Σ(C ⊙ F) / Σ(C). Of every citation the report makes, the fraction landing on a
174	    source that genuinely supports that statement. 0.0 when the report cites nothing."""
175	    return (sum_c_and_f / sum_c) if sum_c else 0.0
176	
177	
178	def citation_thoroughness(sum_c_and_f: int, sum_f: int) -> float:
179	    """Metric VIII — Citation Thoroughness (= recall). HIGHER better.
180	
181	    Formula: Σ(C ⊙ F) / Σ(F). Of every true (statement, source) support pair, the fraction the
182	    report actually cited. 0.0 when F has no support pair."""
183	    return (sum_c_and_f / sum_f) if sum_f else 0.0

187	# Source-cover algorithms
188	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
189	def greedy_min_set_cover(universe: set[int], source_sets: dict[int, set[int]]) -> list[int]:
190	    """Greedy minimum SET cover — the DeepTRACE Source-Necessity numerator (HEADLINE).
191	
192	    Mirrors `third_party/answer-engine-eval/.../utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover` and the paid
193	    `scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.minimum_source_cover_size`: repeatedly take the source
194	    covering the most still-uncovered universe elements. Deterministic tie-break: highest new
195	    coverage, then LOWEST source index. Returns the chosen source indices (cover size = len).
196	    Guards against a non-coverable universe element (breaks on zero gain) so it never hangs."""
197	    covered: set[int] = set()
198	    cover: list[int] = []
199	    while covered != universe:
200	        best_idx: Optional[int] = None
201	        best_gain = 0
202	        for idx in sorted(source_sets):  # sorted => LOWEST-index tie-break is deterministic
203	            gain = len(source_sets[idx] - covered)
204	            if gain > best_gain:
205	                best_gain = gain
206	                best_idx = idx
207	        if best_idx is None or best_gain == 0:
208	            break  # no source covers a remaining element (unreachable when universe is covered)
209	        cover.append(best_idx)
210	        covered |= source_sets[best_idx]
211	    return cover

214	def _hopcroft_karp(adj: dict[int, list[int]], left: list[int], right: list[int]) -> dict[int, int]:
215	    """Hopcroft-Karp maximum bipartite matching. Returns match_right {right_vertex: left_vertex}.
216	
217	    `adj[u]` = list of right vertices adjacent to left vertex u. `left`/`right` = vertex id lists.
218	    Standard BFS-layered / DFS-augment implementation; O(E·sqrt(V))."""
219	    import collections
220	
221	    INF = float("inf")
222	    match_left: dict[int, Optional[int]] = {u: None for u in left}
223	    match_right: dict[int, Optional[int]] = {v: None for v in right}
224	    dist: dict[int, float] = {}
225	
226	    def bfs() -> bool:
227	        queue: "collections.deque[int]" = collections.deque()
228	        for u in left:
229	            if match_left[u] is None:
230	                dist[u] = 0
231	                queue.append(u)
232	            else:
233	                dist[u] = INF
234	        found = False
235	        while queue:
236	            u = queue.popleft()
237	            for v in adj.get(u, []):
238	                w = match_right[v]
239	                if w is None:
240	                    found = True
241	                elif dist[w] == INF:
242	                    dist[w] = dist[u] + 1
243	                    queue.append(w)
244	        return found
245	
246	    def dfs(u: int) -> bool:
247	        for v in adj.get(u, []):
248	            w = match_right[v]
249	            if w is None or (dist[w] == dist[u] + 1 and dfs(w)):
250	                match_left[u] = v
251	                match_right[v] = u
252	                return True
253	        dist[u] = INF
254	        return False
255	
256	    while bfs():
257	        for u in left:
258	            if match_left[u] is None:
259	                dfs(u)
260	    return {v: u for v, u in match_right.items() if u is not None}
261	
262	
263	def konig_min_vertex_cover(
264	    adj: dict[int, list[int]], left: list[int], right: list[int]
265	) -> tuple[set[int], set[int]]:
266	    """König's theorem: minimum VERTEX cover of a bipartite graph from a max matching.
267	
268	    DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (not the headline Source-Necessity — see the module docstring). Returns
269	    (cover_left, cover_right): a minimum vertex cover whose total size == max-matching size.
270	    Construction: let U = unmatched left vertices; alternating-walk (unmatched edges L→R, matched
271	    edges R→L) marks reachable Z; cover = (left − Z_left) ∪ (right ∩ Z_right)."""
272	    match_right = _hopcroft_karp(adj, left, right)          # {right: left}
273	    match_left = {u: v for v, u in match_right.items()}      # {left: right}
274	
275	    z_left: set[int] = set()
276	    z_right: set[int] = set()
277	    stack = [u for u in left if u not in match_left]         # unmatched left vertices
278	    z_left.update(stack)
279	    while stack:
280	        u = stack.pop()
281	        for v in adj.get(u, []):
282	            if v in z_right:
283	                continue
284	            # traverse an edge NOT in the matching (L→R)
285	            if match_left.get(u) == v:
286	                continue
287	            z_right.add(v)
288	            w = match_right.get(v)  # follow the matched edge R→L
289	            if w is not None and w not in z_left:
290	                z_left.add(w)
291	                stack.append(w)
292	    cover_left = {u for u in left if u not in z_left}
293	    cover_right = {v for v in right if v in z_right}
294	    return cover_left, cover_right
295

311	def extract_answer_body(report_md: str) -> str:
312	    """Everything BEFORE the first appendix boundary (## Bibliography / Methods / Contradiction /
313	    References / V30 / horizontal rule). Mirrors pack_drb2.answer_body; local so this is
314	    standalone. Provenance tokens stripped."""
315	    lines = report_md.splitlines()

323	def split_statements(body: str) -> list[str]:
324	    """Decompose the answer body into the finest citation-bearing units (mirrors
325	    pack_deeptrace._split_statements): per markdown line → table cells (split on `|`) → prose
326	    sentences (terminal-punctuation boundary). Heading lines and the redaction placeholder are
327	    dropped. Real DeepTRACE uses a GPT-5 decomposition pass; this deterministic split is the
328	    disclosed triage approximation."""
329	    statements: list[str] = []
330	    for raw in body.splitlines():
331	        line = raw.strip()
332	        if not line or _HEADING_RE.match(line):
333	            continue
334	        # NOTE: the redaction placeholder is dropped at the SENTENCE level below, NOT the line
335	        # level — real POLARIS sections render as ONE multi-sentence line that carries an inline
336	        # redaction sentence, so a line-level drop would delete the whole section's real claims.
337	        units = [c for c in line.split("|") if c.strip()] if line.startswith("|") else [line]
338	        for unit in units:
339	            for seg in _INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE.split(unit):
340	                seg = seg.strip()
341	                if seg and _REDACTION_MARKER not in seg.lower():
342	                    statements.append(seg)
343	    return statements

346	def parse_bibliography(report_md: str) -> dict[int, dict[str, str]]:
347	    """Parse the `## Bibliography` section: {num: {'title', 'url', 'tier'}}. A bibliography entry
348	    line looks like `[N] Title — https://url (tier T1)`. URL/tier are optional (some entries have
349	    an empty URL). Only lines inside the Bibliography section are read."""
350	    refs: dict[int, dict[str, str]] = {}
351	    in_bib = False
352	    for line in report_md.splitlines():
353	        stripped = line.strip()
354	        if _HEADING_RE.match(line):
355	            in_bib = bool(re.match(r"^\s{0,3}##+\s*bibliography\b", line, re.IGNORECASE))
356	            continue
357	        if not in_bib:
358	            continue
359	        m = _BIB_LINE_RE.match(stripped)
360	        if not m:
361	            continue
362	        num = int(m.group(1))
363	        rest = m.group(2).strip()
364	        tier = ""
365	        tm = _BIB_TIER_RE.search(rest)
366	        if tm:
367	            tier = tm.group(1).strip()
368	            rest = rest[: tm.start()].strip()
369	        url_m = _URL_RE.search(rest)
370	        url = url_m.group(0).rstrip(").,") if url_m else ""
371	        title = rest
372	        if url:
373	            title = rest[: url_m.start()].strip()
374	        title = title.rstrip("—-–:").strip()
375	        refs[num] = {"title": title, "url": url, "tier": tier}
376	    return refs
377

379	def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
380	    u = (url or "").strip().lower()
381	    u = re.sub(r"^https?://", "", u)
382	    u = re.sub(r"^www\.", "", u)
383	    return u.rstrip("/")
384	
385	
386	def _normalize_title(title: str) -> str:
387	    t = (title or "").strip().lower().rstrip("….")
388	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", t)

391	def link_sources_to_spans(
392	    bibliography: dict[int, dict[str, str]], evidence: Sequence[dict]
393	) -> dict[int, Optional[str]]:
394	    """Resolve each bibliography number to its snapshot `direct_quote` span via normalized URL
395	    match first, then normalized title match. Unresolved → None (an `n_unreachable` source that
396	    can never contribute F-support). Purely deterministic."""
397	    by_url: dict[str, str] = {}
398	    by_title: dict[str, str] = {}
399	    for ev in evidence:
400	        span = (ev.get("direct_quote") or "").strip()
401	        if not span:
402	            continue
403	        u = _normalize_url(ev.get("source_url") or "")
404	        if u and u not in by_url:
405	            by_url[u] = span
406	        t = _normalize_title(ev.get("title") or ev.get("statement") or "")
407	        if t and t not in by_title:
408	            by_title[t] = span
409	    span_by_num: dict[int, Optional[str]] = {}
410	    for num, ref in bibliography.items():
411	        span = None
412	        u = _normalize_url(ref.get("url") or "")
413	        if u and u in by_url:
414	            span = by_url[u]
415	        if span is None:
416	            t = _normalize_title(ref.get("title") or "")
417	            if t and t in by_title:
418	                span = by_title[t]
419	        span_by_num[num] = span
420	    return span_by_num

423	def citation_columns(statements: Sequence[str]) -> list[set[int]]:
424	    """Per statement, the SET of source numbers it cites (parsed from `[N]` / `[N, M]`)."""
425	    out: list[set[int]] = []
426	    for s in statements:
427	        nums: set[int] = set()
428	        for grp in _CITE_RE.findall(s):
429	            for tok in grp.split(","):
430	                tok = tok.strip()
431	                if tok.isdigit():
432	                    nums.add(int(tok))
433	        out.append(nums)
434	    return out

438	# Default entailment wrapper — reuses the POLARIS NLI engine (lazy import)
439	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
440	def _default_entail_fn(premise_span: str, hypothesis_statement: str) -> Optional[bool]:
441	    """Production F-matrix verdict: does the source SPAN entail the STATEMENT? Reuses
442	    `src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli.entails_directional` (the ALCE / DeepTRACE
443	    citation direction — span→claim SUPPORT, forward logits only). Imported LAZILY so importing
444	    this script / running `--help` never loads torch. Returns True / False / None (None on any
445	    infra fault; the caller counts it as F=0 and tallies n_unknown_entailment)."""
446	    repo_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
447	    if str(repo_root) not in sys.path:
448	        sys.path.insert(0, str(repo_root))
449	    try:
450	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli import entails_directional
451	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — engine unavailable => UNKNOWN verdict, never crash
452	        print(f"[deeptrace_self_score] NLI engine unavailable ({exc}); F verdicts = UNKNOWN.",
453	              file=sys.stderr)
454	        return None
455	    try:
456	        return entails_directional(premise_span, hypothesis_statement)
457	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a verdict failure is UNKNOWN, never fatal (triage)
458	        print(f"[deeptrace_self_score] entailment failed ({exc}); verdict = UNKNOWN.",
459	              file=sys.stderr)
460	        return None

466	def compute_deeptrace_selfscore(
467	    *,
468	    statements: Sequence[str],
469	    bibliography: dict[int, dict[str, str]],
470	    span_by_num: dict[int, Optional[str]],
471	    entail_fn: EntailFn,
472	    is_debate: bool = False,
473	    has_pro: Optional[bool] = None,
474	    has_con: Optional[bool] = None,
475	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
476	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
477	) -> dict[str, Any]:
478	    """Build the Citation matrix C and Factual-support matrix F from parsed inputs, then compute
479	    all 8 DeepTRACE metrics. PURE and offline; NEVER raises on degenerate/empty input (returns
480	    zero-filled metrics). `entail_fn(span, statement)` supplies each F verdict (stubbed in tests).
481	    """
482	    listed_nums = sorted(bibliography)
483	    n_listed = len(listed_nums)
484	    col_of = {num: k for k, num in enumerate(listed_nums)}
485	    n_stmt = len(statements)
486	
487	    cite_sets = citation_columns(statements)
488	    # Core/relevant heuristic: a statement is core iff it carries ≥1 citation to a LISTED source.
489	    relevant = [bool(cs & set(listed_nums)) for cs in cite_sets]
490	    n_relevant = sum(relevant)
491	
492	    # Citation matrix C (only citations to listed sources).
493	    C = [[0] * n_listed for _ in range(n_stmt)]
494	    for i, cs in enumerate(cite_sets):
495	        for num in cs:
496	            if num in col_of:
497	                C[i][col_of[num]] = 1
498	
499	    # Factual-support matrix F — F[i][j]=1 iff span j entails statement i (citation markup
500	    # stripped from the hypothesis). Computed over EVERY span-resolvable listed source, not just
501	    # cited ones (DeepTRACE judges support independent of citation).
502	    F = [[0] * n_listed for _ in range(n_stmt)]
503	    n_unknown_entailment = 0
504	    hypotheses = [strip_citation_markup(s) for s in statements]
505	    for j, num in enumerate(listed_nums):
506	        span = span_by_num.get(num)
507	        if not span:
508	            continue  # unreachable source: no span => contributes no F-support
509	        for i in range(n_stmt):
510	            hyp = hypotheses[i]
511	            if not hyp:
512	                continue
513	            verdict = entail_fn(span, hyp)
514	            if verdict is None:
515	                n_unknown_entailment += 1
516	            elif verdict:
517	                F[i][j] = 1
518	
519	    n_unreachable = sum(1 for num in listed_nums if not span_by_num.get(num))
520	
521	    # ── Metric III: Relevant Statement ────────────────────────────────────
522	    m_relevant = relevant_statement_ratio(n_relevant, n_stmt)
523	
524	    # ── Metric IV: Uncited Sources ────────────────────────────────────────
525	    cited_cols = {col_of[num] for cs in cite_sets for num in cs if num in col_of}
526	    n_cited = len(cited_cols)
527	    m_uncited = uncited_sources_ratio(n_cited, n_listed)
528	    cited_fraction = (n_cited / n_listed) if n_listed else 0.0
529	
530	    # ── Metric V: Unsupported Statements (over relevant statements) ────────
531	    n_unsupported = 0
532	    for i in range(n_stmt):
533	        if relevant[i] and not any(F[i]):
534	            n_unsupported += 1
535	    m_unsupported = unsupported_statements_ratio(n_unsupported, n_relevant)
536	
537	    # ── Metrics VII / VIII: Citation Accuracy / Thoroughness ──────────────
538	    sum_c = sum(sum(row) for row in C)
539	    sum_f = sum(sum(row) for row in F)
540	    sum_cf = sum(C[i][j] & F[i][j] for i in range(n_stmt) for j in range(n_listed))
541	    m_accuracy = citation_accuracy(sum_cf, sum_c)
542	    m_thoroughness = citation_thoroughness(sum_cf, sum_f)
543	
544	    # ── Metric VI: Source Necessity ───────────────────────────────────────
545	    # Universe = supported relevant statements; source_sets[j] = supported relevant statements j
546	    # supports. HEADLINE = greedy min-set-cover; DIAGNOSTIC = Hopcroft-Karp→König min-vertex-cover.
547	    universe: set[int] = set()
548	    source_sets: dict[int, set[int]] = {j: set() for j in range(n_listed)}
549	    for i in range(n_stmt):
550	        if not relevant[i]:
551	            continue
552	        supporters = [j for j in range(n_listed) if F[i][j]]
553	        if not supporters:
554	            continue
555	        universe.add(i)
556	        for j in supporters:
557	            source_sets[j].add(i)
558	    setcover = greedy_min_set_cover(universe, source_sets)
559	    setcover_size = len(setcover)
560	    m_necessity = source_necessity_ratio(setcover_size, n_listed)
561	    n_sole_supporter = sum(
562	        1 for j in range(n_listed)
563	        if any(len([k for k in range(n_listed) if F[i][k]]) == 1 and F[i][j]
564	               for i in range(n_stmt) if relevant[i])
565	    )
566	    # Diagnostic min-vertex-cover (König) over the same bipartite support graph.
567	    left = sorted(universe)
568	    right = [j for j in range(n_listed) if source_sets[j]]
569	    adj = {i: [j for j in range(n_listed) if F[i][j] and source_sets[j]] for i in left}
570	    cover_left, cover_right = konig_min_vertex_cover(adj, left, right)
571	    mvc_size = len(cover_left) + len(cover_right)
572	    m_necessity_mvc = source_necessity_ratio(mvc_size, n_listed)
573	
574	    # ── Metrics I / II: debate-only ───────────────────────────────────────
575	    one_sided: Optional[int] = None
576	    overconfident: Optional[int] = None
577	    if is_debate and has_pro is not None and has_con is not None:
578	        one_sided = one_sided_answer(bool(has_pro), bool(has_con))
579	        overconfident = overconfident_answer(one_sided, answer_confidence, statement_confidence)
580	
581	    return {
582	        "scorer": "polaris-offline-deeptrace-self-score",
583	        "role": "TRIAGE_PREDICTOR_ONLY",
584	        "is_estimate": True,
585	        "is_pass_fail_gate": False,
586	        "n_statements": n_stmt,
587	        "n_relevant_statements": n_relevant,
588	        "n_listed_sources": n_listed,
589	        "n_cited_sources": n_cited,
590	        "n_unreachable_sources": n_unreachable,
591	        "n_unknown_entailment_verdicts": n_unknown_entailment,
592	        # lower-better
593	        "one_sided": one_sided,
594	        "overconfident": overconfident,
595	        "uncited_sources_ratio": round(m_uncited, 4),
596	        "cited_sources_fraction": round(cited_fraction, 4),
597	        "unsupported_statements_ratio": round(m_unsupported, 4),
598	        # higher-better
599	        "relevant_statements_ratio": round(m_relevant, 4),
600	        # The paid path uses a SUPPLIED per-statement relevant[] bool from the GPT-5 core/filler
601	        # decomposition (deeptrace_scorer.py:149-152); that is NOT available offline, so this
602	        # ratio is an offline citation-presence PROXY that WILL diverge from the paid value.
603	        "relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy": True,
604	        "source_necessity": round(m_necessity, 4),
605	        "source_necessity_cover_size": setcover_size,
606	        "source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic": round(m_necessity_mvc, 4),
607	        "source_necessity_mvc_size": mvc_size,
608	        "n_sole_supporter": n_sole_supporter,
609	        "citation_accuracy": round(m_accuracy, 4),
610	        "citation_thoroughness": round(m_thoroughness, 4),
611	        # matrices sums (for auditability)
612	        "sum_C": sum_c,
613	        "sum_F": sum_f,
614	        "sum_C_and_F": sum_cf,
615	        # honesty / provenance
616	        "source_necessity_interpretation": (
617	            "HEADLINE source_necessity = greedy min-set-cover / n_listed (matches the paid "
618	            "deeptrace_scorer.py + official utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover). "
619	            "source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic = Hopcroft-Karp->Konig min-vertex-cover / n_listed "
620	            "(the algorithm the Wave-1c task names; over-states necessity on redundant reports, "
621	            "so kept as a labeled diagnostic, NOT the headline)."
622	        ),
623	        "honest_limitation": (
624	            "F-matrix is SPAN-APPROXIMATE: judged over the snapshot direct_quote span, not the "
625	            "source's full text (~550/694 spans truncated). This UNDER-estimates support => "
626	            "OVER-estimates Unsupported, UNDER-estimates Citation-Thoroughness. "
627	            f"{n_unreachable} listed source(s) resolved to NO span (unreachable; deflate "
628	            "Source-Necessity). Relevant-Statement uses an offline citation-presence proxy for "
629	            "the paid GPT-5 core/filler relevant[] labels, which are unavailable offline; this "
630	            "ratio will diverge from the paid Relevant-Statement and is the least-reliable triage "
631	            "estimate (see relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy=true). One-Sided/Overconfident need "
632	            "an offline stance+confidence judge we do not run (None unless supplied). "
633	            "Scores the full bibliography (no S1..S10 source cap), matching the named paid scorer "
634	            "deeptrace_scorer.py (which has no cap); the official EXTERNAL harness caps at 10 "
635	            "sources, so on reports citing >10 sources the Uncited-Sources and Source-Necessity "
636	            "ratios may differ from that external harness (Citation-Accuracy/Thoroughness are "
637	            "cap-insensitive). "
638	            "TRIAGE PREDICTION, NOT a proven score and NOT a pass/fail gate."
639	        ),
640	    }
641	
642	
643	def score_report(
644	    report_path: str | Path,
645	    snapshot_path: str | Path,
646	    *,
647	    entail_fn: Optional[EntailFn] = None,
648	    is_debate: bool = False,
649	    has_pro: Optional[bool] = None,
650	    has_con: Optional[bool] = None,
651	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
652	) -> dict[str, Any]:
653	    """Parse a rendered report.md + banked corpus_snapshot.json and return the triage metrics.
654	    `entail_fn=None` uses the production NLI engine (lazy). Never raises: on any read/parse error
655	    it returns a zero-filled result carrying an `error` note (triage must never block)."""
656	    ef = entail_fn or _default_entail_fn
657	    try:
658	        report_md = Path(report_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
659	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
660	        report_md = ""
661	        _err = f"could not read report: {exc}"
662	    else:
663	        _err = ""
664	    try:
665	        snap = json.loads(Path(snapshot_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"))
666	        evidence = snap.get("evidence_for_gen", []) if isinstance(snap, dict) else []
667	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
668	        evidence = []
669	        _err = (_err + "; " if _err else "") + f"could not read snapshot: {exc}"
670	
671	    bibliography = parse_bibliography(report_md)
672	    statements = split_statements(extract_answer_body(report_md))
673	    span_by_num = link_sources_to_spans(bibliography, evidence)
674	    result = compute_deeptrace_selfscore(
675	        statements=statements,
676	        bibliography=bibliography,
677	        span_by_num=span_by_num,
678	        entail_fn=ef,
679	        is_debate=is_debate,
680	        has_pro=has_pro,
681	        has_con=has_con,
682	        answer_confidence=answer_confidence,
683	    )
684	    if _err:
685	        result["error"] = _err
686	    return result
687	
688	
689	_TRIAGE_HEADER = (
690	    "================================================================\n"
691	    " POLARIS offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY\n"
692	    " These numbers are ESTIMATES (span-approximate), NOT a proven score.\n"
693	    " This is NOT a pass/fail gate: it never blocks, aborts, or decides.\n"
694	    " Relevant-Statement is an offline citation-presence PROXY for the paid\n"
695	    " GPT-5 core/filler labels (unavailable offline) — the least-reliable line.\n"
696	    " Run it to predict the paid DeepTRACE judge cheaply, before spending.\n"
697	    "================================================================"
698	)
699	
700	
701	def _plain_summary(m: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
702	    def fmt(v: Any) -> str:
703	        return "n/a" if v is None else (f"{v:.3f}" if isinstance(v, float) else str(v))
704	    lines = [
705	        "Plain summary (higher/lower-better noted; all estimates):",
706	        f"  Relevant-Statement (higher better):   {fmt(m['relevant_statements_ratio'])}",
707	        f"  Uncited-Sources    (lower better):    {fmt(m['uncited_sources_ratio'])}",
708	        f"  Unsupported-Stmts  (lower better):    {fmt(m['unsupported_statements_ratio'])}",
709	        f"  Source-Necessity   (higher better):   {fmt(m['source_necessity'])}"
710	        f"   [mvc-diagnostic {fmt(m['source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic'])}]",
711	        f"  Citation-Accuracy  (higher better):   {fmt(m['citation_accuracy'])}",
712	        f"  Citation-Thorough. (higher better):   {fmt(m['citation_thoroughness'])}",
713	        f"  One-Sided/Overconf (debate, lower):   {fmt(m['one_sided'])} / {fmt(m['overconfident'])}",
714	        f"  statements={m['n_statements']} relevant={m['n_relevant_statements']} "
715	        f"listed_sources={m['n_listed_sources']} unreachable={m['n_unreachable_sources']} "
716	        f"unknown_entailment={m['n_unknown_entailment_verdicts']}",
717	    ]
718	    return "\n".join(lines)
719	
720	
721	def main(argv: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None) -> int:
722	    """CLI. Prints the triage header, the metrics JSON, and a plain summary. NEVER exits non-zero
723	    on a scoring problem (triage must not block a pipeline); returns 0 always for scoring paths."""
724	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
725	        description=(
726	            "Offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY (not a pass/fail gate). "
727	            "Estimates a POLARIS report's 8 DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness metrics from a "
728	            "rendered report.md + a banked corpus_snapshot.json, before paid GPT-5 judging. "
729	            "F-matrix is span-approximate by construction (see the module docstring)."
730	        )
731	    )
732	    ap.add_argument("--report", help="path to the rendered report.md")
733	    ap.add_argument("--snapshot", help="path to the banked corpus_snapshot.json")
734	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="", help="optional path to write the metrics JSON")
735	    ap.add_argument("--is-debate", action="store_true",
736	                    help="treat as a debate query (One-Sided/Overconfident need --has-pro/--has-con)")
737	    ap.add_argument("--has-pro", action="store_true", help="a supported pro statement is present")
738	    ap.add_argument("--has-con", action="store_true", help="a supported con statement is present")
739	    ap.add_argument("--confidence", type=int, default=None,
740	                    help="optional whole-answer 1-5 confidence for the Overconfident metric")
741	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
742	
743	    print(_TRIAGE_HEADER)
744	    if not args.report or not args.snapshot:
745	        print("\nProvide --report and --snapshot to score. Example:\n"
746	              "  python scripts/deeptrace_self_score.py --report outputs/.../report.md "
747	              "--snapshot outputs/.../corpus_snapshot.json\n"
748	              "(--help for all options). Nothing scored; triage predictor is a no-op here.")
749	        return 0
750	
751	    result = score_report(
752	        args.report, args.snapshot,
753	        is_debate=args.is_debate,
754	        has_pro=args.has_pro if args.is_debate else None,
755	        has_con=args.has_con if args.is_debate else None,
756	        answer_confidence=args.confidence,
757	    )
758	    print("\n" + json.dumps(result, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
759	    print("\n" + _plain_summary(result))
760	    if args.out:
761	        try:
762	            Path(args.out).write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False),
763	                                      encoding="utf-8")
764	            print(f"\n[deeptrace_self_score] wrote {args.out}")
765	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a write failure must not fail the triage run
766	            print(f"\n[deeptrace_self_score] could not write --out ({exc}).", file=sys.stderr)
767	    return 0
768	
769	
770	if __name__ == "__main__":
771	    raise SystemExit(main())
